rationale: CIC-DUX4 directly activates the ETV1/4/5 paralog panel. ETV1 elevated in CIC-rearranged sarcoma.

DUX4 is normally silenced in somatic tissue; CIC-DUX4 fusion expresses the DUX4 portion.
CCNB3 is normally testis-restricted; expression in a small-blue-round-cell sarcoma is near-pathognomonic.
BEND2 is normally silent in the CNS; its expression signals MN1-BEND2 fusion astroblastoma.
ZFTA-RELA drives L1CAM expression via NFκB activation; L1CAM is a pathognomonic IHC + expression marker for RELA-fusion ependymoma.
NFκB target activated by ZFTA-RELA.
ALK is normally silent post-development; expression >1 TPM plus an epithelial/mesenchymal context signals ALK rearrangement.
NTRK1 is normally restricted to neural tissue; ectopic expression in other lineages signals NTRK1 fusion. TRK-inhibitor eligible (larotrectinib / entrectinib).
Same — ectopic NTRK2 signals NTRK2 fusion.
Same — ectopic NTRK3 signals NTRK3 (most common TRK fusion partner).
ROS1 is normally silent post-development; expression signals rearrangement.
RET expression is normally low outside neural crest derivatives; elevated RET signals fusion or (in MTC) germline/somatic point mutation.
Total ABL1 mRNA is not a reliable surrogate for BCR-ABL1; fusion-specific transcript testing is required.
source_anchor: PMID supports fused transcript detection, not total ABL1 expression as a surrogate
